Reason: Best for showing continuous trends over time
EvaluationTask: Identify the month with highest revenue
RecommendationReason: Line charts reveal trend direction and momentum instantly for time series data
AlternativeReason: Use when exact monthly value comparison matters more than trend
AvoidReason: Pie charts cannot show time progression

Reason: Side-by-side comparison across regions
EvaluationTask: Which region had lowest sales in Q3?
RecommendationReason: Bar charts enable instant visual comparison between discrete categories
AlternativeReason: Use when comparing across multiple years simultaneously
AvoidReason: Line charts imply continuity between regions which does not exist

Reason: Hierarchical view of category contributions
EvaluationTask: Which sub-category contributed most to revenue?
RecommendationReason: Treemaps show hierarchical part-to-whole relationships with better space usage than pie
AlternativeReason: Use only when fewer than 5 categories exist
AvoidReason: Bar charts do not show part-to-whole relationships clearly

Reason: Shows cumulative growth and volume simultaneously
EvaluationTask: In which quarter did growth exceed 20%?
RecommendationReason: Area charts emphasize volume and cumulative magnitude better than line charts
AlternativeReason: Use when exact trend values matter more than volume
AvoidReason: Bar charts hide the cumulative growth story

Reason: Three dimensions encoded simultaneously
EvaluationTask: Which segment is most profitable per sale?
RecommendationReason: Bubble charts encode 3 variables simultaneously — position and size
AlternativeReason: Use when size dimension is not critical
AvoidReason: Bar charts can only show 1-2 dimensions effectively

Reason: Direct comparison of same period across years
EvaluationTask: Which month showed biggest YoY growth?
RecommendationReason: Grouped bars make same-period cross-year comparison immediately visible
AlternativeReason: Use when trend continuity across years matters more
AvoidReason: Stacked bars hide individual year values making comparison hard

Reason: Rankings are clearest on horizontal axis with readable labels
EvaluationTask: Which top 5 sub-categories drove 80% of revenue?
RecommendationReason: Horizontal bars allow long category labels and make ranking instantly scannable
AlternativeReason: Use only when fewer than 6 categories with short names
AvoidReason: Pie charts make ranking comparison very difficult

Reason: Center space shows total while segments show proportion
EvaluationTask: What share of sales came from Corporate vs Consumer?
RecommendationReason: Donut charts improve on pie by using center space for key metric display
AlternativeReason: Acceptable when no center metric is needed
AvoidReason: Bar charts do not convey part-to-whole relationship as intuitively

Reason: Reveals correlation strength and direction with outliers
EvaluationTask: Does higher discount consistently reduce profit?
RecommendationReason: Scatter plots with trendline show correlation and individual data point outliers
AlternativeReason: Use only when data is ordered time series not scattered
AvoidReason: Bar charts cannot show relationships between two continuous variables

Reason: Spatial patterns visible instantly on geographic map
EvaluationTask: Which states have highest sales concentration?
RecommendationReason: Choropleth maps leverage spatial memory — users instantly recognize geographic patterns
AlternativeReason: Use when exact state values matter more than geographic pattern
AvoidReason: Pie charts completely lose geographic context

Reason: 100% normalization shows proportion change over time
EvaluationTask: Is express shipping adoption growing year over year?
RecommendationReason: Normalized stacked bars show proportional composition change across time periods
AlternativeReason: Use when absolute values matter more than proportions
AvoidReason: Separate pie charts per year make comparison very difficult

Reason: Multiple KPIs compared across entities on single chart
EvaluationTask: Which region is strongest across all dimensions?
RecommendationReason: Radar charts show multi-dimensional performance profiles at a glance
AlternativeReason: Use when comparing fewer than 4 dimensions
AvoidReason: Line charts cannot show multi-dimensional comparison effectively

Reason: Color intensity reveals patterns across two categorical dimensions
EvaluationTask: Which department-role has highest attrition?
RecommendationReason: Heatmaps reveal patterns across two categorical dimensions simultaneously using color
AlternativeReason: Use when exact attrition counts matter more than pattern
AvoidReason: Pie charts cannot show two-dimensional categorical patterns

Reason: Shows frequency distribution shape skew and spread
EvaluationTask: What percentage score below 3 on satisfaction?
RecommendationReason: Histograms reveal distribution shape — skew peak and spread — which tables hide completely
AlternativeReason: Acceptable for discrete ordinal data with few categories
AvoidReason: Line charts imply continuity in categorical satisfaction scores

Reason: Instant at-a-glance monitoring with delta comparison
EvaluationTask: What is the current overall attrition rate?
RecommendationReason: KPI cards deliver single critical metrics with maximum speed — no chart interpretation needed
AlternativeReason: Use when target threshold visualization is needed
AvoidReason: Bar charts add unnecessary complexity for single metric monitoring

Reason: Proportional composition within binary groups
EvaluationTask: Is overtime a major driver of attrition?
RecommendationReason: Stacked bars show attrition proportion within overtime vs non-overtime groups clearly
AlternativeReason: Use when absolute counts matter more than proportions
AvoidReason: Separate pies make direct comparison between groups difficult

Reason: Shows median quartiles and outliers for each group
EvaluationTask: Which department has youngest average workforce?
RecommendationReason: Box plots show full distribution summary — median spread and outliers — in compact form
AlternativeReason: Use only when mean comparison is sufficient
AvoidReason: Histograms cannot compare distributions across multiple groups efficiently

Reason: Full distribution shape with embedded box plot summary
EvaluationTask: Is there a salary gap between job levels?
RecommendationReason: Violin plots show full distribution shape revealing bimodal or skewed patterns box plots miss
AlternativeReason: Use when distribution shape is less important than quartiles
AvoidReason: Bar charts show only mean hiding salary distribution complexity

Reason: Individual data points reveal compensation fairness patterns
EvaluationTask: Are top performers compensated fairly?
RecommendationReason: Scatter plots reveal individual outliers — underpaid high performers instantly visible
AlternativeReason: Use when group averages are sufficient
AvoidReason: Line charts imply ordering in performance ratings which is misleading

Reason: Shows progressive reduction across satisfaction levels
EvaluationTask: How many employees rate work-life balance as poor?
RecommendationReason: Funnel charts show drop-off between satisfaction levels revealing where employees cluster
AlternativeReason: Acceptable for simple frequency comparison
AvoidReason: Pie charts do not convey the progressive nature of satisfaction levels

Reason: Trendline quantifies training ROI relationship
EvaluationTask: Does more training lead to higher performance?
RecommendationReason: Scatter with trendline shows both individual variation and overall training-performance relationship
AlternativeReason: Use when comparing average performance per training group
AvoidReason: Line charts cannot show scattered individual employee data points

Reason: Combined instant overview with detailed breakdown
EvaluationTask: Which department is most understaffed?
RecommendationReason: KPI cards give instant totals while bar chart provides department-level detail simultaneously
AlternativeReason: Use when exact numbers are more important than visual comparison
AvoidReason: Pie charts make headcount comparison across departments less precise

Reason: Reveals most common tenure before leaving
EvaluationTask: What is most common tenure before employees leave?
RecommendationReason: Histograms reveal tenure distribution peaks showing when attrition risk is highest
AlternativeReason: Use when median and quartile comparison across groups is needed
AvoidReason: Bar charts on continuous tenure data lose distribution shape information

Reason: Direct satisfaction comparison across all job roles
EvaluationTask: Which job role has lowest average satisfaction?
RecommendationReason: Bar charts make direct comparison between many categories instantly scannable
AlternativeReason: Line charts imply trend between roles which does not exist
AvoidReason: Radar charts become cluttered with many job role categories

Reason: Distribution comparison reveals pay gap evidence
EvaluationTask: Is there a statistically visible income gap by gender?
RecommendationReason: Box plots by gender group reveal median differences and overlap showing pay equity clearly
AlternativeReason: Use only when mean comparison is legally sufficient
AvoidReason: Scatter plots without grouping hide the gender dimension

Reason: Day and month patterns reveal absence seasonality
EvaluationTask: Which month has highest absenteeism?
RecommendationReason: Calendar heatmaps leverage time pattern recognition — seasonal absence instantly visible
AlternativeReason: Use when monthly trend over time matters more than day patterns
AvoidReason: Bar charts cannot show two-dimensional time patterns simultaneously

Reason: Cumulative positive and negative variances shown step by step
EvaluationTask: In which month did profit deviate most from budget?
RecommendationReason: Waterfall charts show cumulative variance build-up — instantly shows where budget went wrong
AlternativeReason: Use when monthly variance comparison matters more than cumulative effect
AvoidReason: Line charts do not clearly show positive vs negative variance separation

Reason: Trend line against target threshold shows performance gaps
EvaluationTask: When did profit margin fall below 10% threshold?
RecommendationReason: Threshold line on trend chart immediately highlights when performance fell below target
AlternativeReason: Use when margin volume matters as much as trend
AvoidReason: Bar charts make threshold crossing less visually obvious

Reason: Monthly cost and revenue components compared simultaneously
EvaluationTask: Which month had highest discount-to-revenue ratio?
RecommendationReason: Stacked bars show both total and component breakdown across months simultaneously
AlternativeReason: Use when trend of individual components matters more than composition
AvoidReason: Separate pie charts per month make comparison very difficult

Reason: Actual trend extended with uncertainty range shown
EvaluationTask: What is projected Q4 revenue based on trend?
RecommendationReason: Confidence bands show forecast uncertainty — executives see both projection and risk range
AlternativeReason: Use when showing only point forecast without uncertainty
AvoidReason: Scatter plots cannot show time-ordered forecast progression clearly

Reason: Profit ranking immediately scannable with readable labels
EvaluationTask: Which category has lowest profit margin?
RecommendationReason: Horizontal bars make profit ranking instantly scannable — negative profit visible below zero line
AlternativeReason: Use only when fewer than 5 categories with short names
AvoidReason: Pie charts cannot show negative profit values

Reason: Discount level vs profit loss relationship with outliers
EvaluationTask: What discount level causes profit to turn negative?
RecommendationReason: Scatter plots reveal the exact discount threshold where profit turns negative
AlternativeReason: Use when average profit per discount band is sufficient
AvoidReason: Bar charts group discount ranges hiding the continuous relationship

Reason: Four financial KPIs on one screen for instant overview
EvaluationTask: What is the overall financial health this quarter?
RecommendationReason: KPI card grids deliver multiple critical metrics simultaneously with maximum speed
AlternativeReason: Use when detailed row-level data is needed
AvoidReason: Bar charts add interpretation overhead for simple summary metrics

Reason: Hierarchical drill-down from total to sub-category expenses
EvaluationTask: Which sub-category incurs most discount cost?
RecommendationReason: Sunburst charts enable hierarchical expense drill-down in single compact visualization
AlternativeReason: Very similar — use treemap when hierarchy depth exceeds 3 levels
AvoidReason: Pie charts cannot show hierarchical expense breakdown

Reason: Actual vs target shown as intuitive dial reading
EvaluationTask: Which region is furthest from hitting sales target?
RecommendationReason: Gauge charts leverage speedometer mental model — target achievement instantly understood
AlternativeReason: Use when comparing multiple regions simultaneously
AvoidReason: KPI cards do not show target proximity as intuitively as gauges

Reason: ROI trend against break-even threshold reveals discount efficiency
EvaluationTask: At what discount level does ROI drop below break-even?
RecommendationReason: Break-even threshold line on ROI chart instantly shows discount efficiency boundary
AlternativeReason: Use when individual transaction ROI matters more than trend
AvoidReason: Bar charts make the continuous ROI-discount relationship less clear

Reason: Overall correlation with category color encoding
EvaluationTask: Does higher sales always guarantee higher profit?
RecommendationReason: Colored scatter with trendline shows both overall correlation and category-level patterns
AlternativeReason: Use only when data is time-ordered not scattered
AvoidReason: Bar charts cannot reveal the continuous relationship between sales and profit

Reason: Low satisfaction as attrition predictor with department color
EvaluationTask: Does low satisfaction strongly predict attrition?
RecommendationReason: Color-encoded scatter reveals which departments cluster at high attrition low satisfaction
AlternativeReason: Use when average satisfaction per attrition group is sufficient
AvoidReason: Pie charts cannot show continuous relationships between variables

Reason: All critical business metrics on single screen
EvaluationTask: What is overall health of business and workforce?
RecommendationReason: KPI grid gives C-suite complete business health picture in under 10 seconds
AlternativeReason: Use when detail and drill-down capability matters more
AvoidReason: Charts alone without summary metrics slow executive decision speed

Reason: Day and month sales patterns reveal seasonality
EvaluationTask: Which days consistently generate highest sales?
RecommendationReason: Calendar heatmaps use spatial memory for time — weekly and seasonal patterns emerge instantly
AlternativeReason: Use when monthly trend matters more than day-of-week patterns
AvoidReason: Bar charts cannot show two-dimensional time patterns simultaneously

Reason: Ranked feature importance shows top attrition drivers
EvaluationTask: What are top 3 factors driving attrition?
RecommendationReason: Horizontal ranked bars make feature importance immediately scannable from top to bottom
AlternativeReason: Use when comparing importance profiles across multiple models
AvoidReason: Tables hide the relative magnitude differences between factors

Reason: Retention rates across hiring cohorts over time
EvaluationTask: Which hiring cohort has worst 2-year retention?
RecommendationReason: Cohort heatmaps reveal which employee batches have systematic retention problems
AlternativeReason: Use when tracking single cohort retention over time
AvoidReason: Bar charts cannot show both cohort and time dimensions simultaneously

Reason: Financial and HR performance compared by region simultaneously
EvaluationTask: Which regions have both low profit and high attrition?
RecommendationReason: Side-by-side bars enable simultaneous financial and HR health comparison by region
AlternativeReason: Use when correlation between profit and attrition is the focus
AvoidReason: Tables make cross-dataset regional comparison much slower

Reason: Order frequency distribution with cumulative percentage
EvaluationTask: What percentage of customers place more than 5 orders?
RecommendationReason: Histogram with CDF answers both distribution shape and percentage threshold questions
AlternativeReason: Use when comparing specific frequency buckets only
AvoidReason: Pie charts cannot show continuous frequency distributions effectively

Reason: Two metrics on shared time axis reveals correlation over time
EvaluationTask: Is revenue growth keeping pace with headcount growth?
RecommendationReason: Dual-axis lines show whether two business metrics move together or diverge over time
AlternativeReason: Use when comparing specific time period values not trends
AvoidReason: Scatter plots lose the time dimension that makes this comparison meaningful
